question.text: When a customer is a legal person, what must a Regulated Entity obtain for identification under the IFSCA Guidelines?

answer.text: Full name, trading name, unique identification number (tax/incorporation/registration number), registered address, date and place of incorporation
Only the company name and registered address
The personal details of all shareholders regardless of ownership percentage

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For legal persons, the RE must obtain: (i) full name and any trading name; (ii) unique identification number (Tax ID, incorporation number, business registration number); (iii) registered or business address and principal place of business; (iv) date of establishment/incorporation/registration; and (v) place of incorporation/registration.
**The Trap:** Both the registered address AND principal place of business are required if they are different. Also, the legal form, constitution, and powers that regulate the entity must also be identified.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Legal person identification is more complex in IFSCA-01 than Series XXIV because IFSC entities deal with international legal structures (SPVs, trusts, holding companies across jurisdictions).
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For legal persons, also identify related parties and connected parties, and remain apprised of changes. This is an ongoing obligation, not just a one-time onboarding requirement.
